data.body: FRAC-24 implementation complete — 3 commits on frac-24-audit-campus. Audit doc at .lattice/notes/audits/campus-audit.md covers 18 typography rows and 9 color rows. Gap appendix: one consolidated italic-aside cluster entry (Campus.tsx:218 plus siblings at 367, 419, 453, 532, 570). The master text-white color row consolidates 40+ JSX sites (text-white + text-white/alpha + decoration-white/alpha + border-white/alpha) by (file + token) grouping per audit-prompt section 5. Tests: typecheck clean, test suite shows only the 4 documented baseline failures (footer FRAC-88 italic, footer Jacquard, navigation mobile labels, neighborhood min-h-screen).

VERDICT: PASS

Reviewed cold against plan at .lattice/plans/task_01KTJQF0GAA9Y3AW4YMS50Q2K2.md. Read audit-prompt.md (sections 4, 5, 6, 7, 10), the FRAC-24 audit doc, audit-gaps.md, the plan, and both source files (CampusPage.tsx, Campus.tsx). Spot-checked 16 rows against source JSX. Verified git diff scope (only .lattice/ files; no src/, DESIGN.md, or audit-prompt.md changes). Verified 3-commit logical structure per plan's commit strategy.

Acceptance checklist:
- [PASS] campus-audit.md exists, follows section-10 structure (page metadata, scope, typography audit, color audit, forward observations, gap appendix).
- [PASS] Both in-scope files represented with typography + color rows.
- [PASS] Out-of-scope files listed in preamble with matching rationale (FractalPattern, Navbar/Footer/FadeIn/MandelbrotIcon, SectorHeader internals, Button component).
- [PASS] House identity stated (campus = `campus`, not `school`).
- [PASS] Inversion check documented (default arrangement, no finding).
- [PASS] Cross-house leak check documented (none found, with full hex enumeration).
- [PASS] Every responsive element lists both mobile + desktop renderings.
- [PASS] Every text-white / text-white/<n> / decoration-white classified NEAR → text-house-campus-light-foreground (master row enumerates 40+ sites).
- [PASS] CampusPage.tsx:8 raw-hex #2E6B4A EXACT → house-campus-light + inline color:#fff NEAR → text-house-campus-light-foreground (combined row).
- [PASS] Campus.tsx:190 SectorHeader prop #1A3A2E EXACT → house-campus-deep.
- [PASS] CampusPage.tsx:9 FractalPattern documented as EXCLUDED.
- [PASS] Campus.tsx:161 bg-black/20 + hover:bg-black/30 NEAR → bg-foreground/<alpha>.
- [PASS] Campus.tsx:173 PhotoPlaceholder bg-white/5 + border-white/10 NEAR → bg-house-campus-light-foreground/5 + border-house-campus-light-foreground/10 + added pairing text.
- [PASS] Campus.tsx:499 blockquote border-white/30 NEAR → border-house-campus-light-foreground/30.
- [PASS] Italic-aside cluster classified GAP and appended as ONE consolidated entry in audit-gaps.md.
- [PASS] All 7 plan worked-example rows appear verbatim in order (typography: 196, 256, 230, 174, body-paragraphs cluster, 218 italic-aside; color: CampusPage.tsx:8, Campus.tsx:185, 174+ master text-white row, 190, 173, 161, 499).
- [PASS] Gap appendix mirrors audit-gaps.md entries; branch-ordering note explains Lab-on-master vs Home-upstream.
- [PASS] No src/ changes; no DESIGN.md changes; no audit-prompt.md changes.

Tests: typecheck=clean. tests=4 documented baseline failures only (footer FRAC-88 italic, footer Jacquard, navigation mobile labels, neighborhood min-h-screen). No regressions.

Spot-check findings (non-blocking, noted for FRAC-25 Apply):
1. Line 570 listed in the italic-aside cluster but source has no `italic` class — line 570 reads `text-sm md:text-base text-white/70 font-light leading-relaxed` (P.S. cancel-membership line). The impl correctly excluded 503 from being italic ("503 footer line is upright so not a member") but missed that 570 is also not italic. The forward observation correctly counts only 5 italic sites (218, 367, 419, 453, 532), so the audit's internal data agrees with reality — the cluster line in the GAP entry just over-counts by 1. Apply (FRAC-25) should treat 570 as a member of the master text-white row, not the italic-aside row.
2. Line 196 row uses the className-line number (196) rather than the opening JSX tag line (195 — `<p` opens on 195 with multi-line attributes). Section 4's "opening-tag line wins" rule technically points at 195. Minor; doesn't affect Apply behavior since line numbers will shift on migration anyway.

Both findings are minor and don't merit rework — the audit's substantive classifications, scope, format adherence, worked-example fidelity, and pairing-rule application are correct. Apply task can resolve via row context (file + token grouping) without ambiguity.

Verdict: PASS. Moving to done.
